name: PBR Mark II Patrol Boat
prompt:
Vietnam War era PBR (Patrol Boat, River) Mark II. US Navy "brown water navy" patrol craft for river operations.

Physical structure (~9.5m long):
- Hull: fiberglass V-hull, fast patrol boat
  - Pointed bow, wide stern
  - Low profile, ~0.8m freeboard
  - Flat deck areas fore and aft
  - Slight flare at bow
- Pilot house: small raised cabin amidships
  - Windshield: flat glass panels (3) with wipers
  - Open top (no roof) or canvas sun cover
  - Helm/steering console inside
  - Radio antenna: whip on side
- Weapons:
  - Forward: twin M2 .50 cal on bow mount (twin barrel setup on pedestal)
  - Aft: single M2 .50 cal on stern pedestal mount
  - Amidships: M60 machine gun mount on each side
- Engine cover: raised engine housing at stern
  - Water jet intakes at stern bottom
- Ceramic armor: flat armor plates on sides of pilot house
- Flag staff: at stern for American flag
- Cleats and fittings: simplified bumpers/cleats along gunwale

Colors: Dark navy green (0x3a4a35), gray hull (0x666660), windshield glass (0x88aaaa), dark weapons (0x2a2a2a), deck (0x555550)
Orientation: Hull on water (Y=0), bow pointing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~9.5m long, ~3.5m wide)
animations: Water rocking: gentle bob and roll, 4s cycle
